purpose: Routing rules consumed by the shared decideRoute() in scripts/factory-routing.mjs (imported by both sasha-skeptic.mjs as the producer and factory-replay.mjs as the replayer — single source of truth). Reading from JSON instead of hardcoded logic makes config changes a versioned data delta (task #18 from frames/codex-goals/factory-fully-autonomous-goal-20260528.md) that factory-learn.mjs can propose and apply deterministically. Every routing DECISION is stamped with this `version` + a content hash on the resulting todo.created / routing.skipped event, so replay can classify a divergence as expected-config-evolution (version differs) vs real-nondeterminism (version matches, decision differs). v2: added type_equals/type_prefix predicates + script_crash_type_to_maya + verifier_check_failed_type_to_maya so failures emitted as type=script.crash / type=verifier.check_failed (not type=behavior.failed) finally enter the flywheel.

rules.name: learned_script_crash_test_behavior_sasha
rules.rationale: factory-learn proposal: sasha had 85.0% success vs maya's 50.0% over 7+4 dispatches (improvement 35pp)

rules.name: canary_probe_authorized
rules.rationale: Canary probes (Gap P): operator-issued end-to-end tests bypass the synthetic short-circuit so the full failure->todo->dispatch->completion loop can be exercised in production. emitCanaryProbe() is the ONLY producer-side path that sets canary_authorized=true; ad-hoc synthetic events do not.

rules.name: synthetic_short_circuit
rules.rationale: Test scaffolding events MUST NOT route. Per the 2026-05-28 contamination incident: an unmarked synthetic todo cascaded into a multi-agent debate costing real $. Field-absent defaults to real (fail-safe). Canary path above is the explicit authorized override.

rules.name: transient_rate_limit_skip
rules.rationale: Sasha pauses the bridge separately. Creating a todo would generate work that the rate limit blocks from completing.

rules.name: transient_network_skip
rules.rationale: Bridge already retries. Todo creation would dedupe-thrash.

rules.name: llm_provider_error_to_sasha
rules.rationale: Provider failure (e.g. invalid auth, 5xx) needs operator-facing investigation.

rules.name: agent_failures_to_sasha
rules.rationale: Agent-quality failures (satisfaction_of_search, etc.) escalate to Sasha for human review.

rules.name: verifier_check_failed_to_maya
rules.rationale: Verifier check failures usually indicate a code-fix is needed. Maya gets the worktree.

rules.name: script_crash_to_maya
rules.rationale: Script crashes are bugs in factory tooling. Maya fixes them.

rules.name: script_crash_type_to_maya
rules.rationale: Crash-guard + Python emit_script_crash write type=script.crash with reason=script.<ExceptionClass> (e.g. script.Error). The reason-based script_crash_to_maya rule above never matches those, so before this rule 9 real crashes were logged but never entered the flywheel. Match on the event TYPE to catch every crash regardless of the exception-class reason.

rules.name: verifier_check_failed_type_to_maya
rules.rationale: Same gap as crashes: verifier.check_failed is sometimes emitted as type=verifier.check_failed (not type=behavior.failed reason=verifier.check_failed). Match on type so the verifier's hard failures always reach Maya.

rules.name: phoenix_dispatch_failed_to_sasha
rules.rationale: Meta-flywheel: dispatch failures may indicate Pentagon REST issues. Sasha investigates.

rules.name: llm_stream_parse_error_to_sasha
rules.rationale: H11: a dispatch that exited 0 but produced no parseable final message is a real silent-drop defect in the stream/dispatch layer — Sasha investigates (p1), not a transient skip.

rules.name: llm_timeout_to_sasha
rules.rationale: H10: SIGTERM timeout kills are distinct from network errors. Surfaced at p2 (visible triage) rather than skipped, because a recurring timeout usually means the deadline is too short for the task. Dedup + circuit breaker bound the cost.

rules.name: infrastructure_to_sasha
rules.rationale: Infrastructure events (no-trigger, ghost completion, etc.) are Pentagon-side; Sasha aggregates.

rules.name: default_to_sasha_p2
rules.rationale: Catch-all: unrecognized failure reasons go to Sasha for triage. p2 means tomorrow-not-today.

rule.name: unique identifier for the rule (used in audit logs)
rule.when: predicate object — matched in order, first match wins
rule.skip_todo: true if matched failures should NOT create a todo
rule.rationale: free-text — surfaced in logs when this rule fires
